scenario: Draft a plaint for recovery of Rs.15,00,000 given as a hand loan. The plaintiff Ram Kumar, aged 45, resident of Jayanagar, Bengaluru, advanced Rs.15,00,000 to the defendant Suresh Patel, aged 38, resident of Koramangala, Bengaluru, on 15.03.2024 via NEFT bank transfer (UTR: AXIB20240315123456). There was no written agreement. The defendant acknowledged the loan via WhatsApp messages. Despite repeated oral demands and a legal notice dated 01.07.2024, the defendant has failed to repay. The plaintiff claims interest at 12% per annum from the date of default (20.06.2024). File in City Civil Court, Bengaluru.

scorecard.detail: At least one statutory section cited

draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE District Court / Civil Court
AT Bengaluru

O.S. No. __________ / 20____

Ram Kumar,
Aged about 45 years,
Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Jayanagar, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

                    VERSUS

Suresh Patel,
Aged about 38 years,
Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Koramangala, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR RECOVERY OF MONEY LENT / ADVANCE PAID WITH INTEREST AND COSTS

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

JURISDICTION

1. The Plaintiff is a person sui juris and competent to institute the present suit. The Defendant is also a person sui juris and can be sued.

2. This Hon'ble Court has territorial jurisdiction to try and entertain the present suit inasmuch as the cause of action arose within the local limits of the jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court at Bengaluru, and the Defendant carries on business / resides within such territorial limits.

3. This Hon'ble Court has pecuniary jurisdiction to try the present suit, the suit being valued at Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only), which is within the pecuniary limits prescribed for this Hon'ble Court.

LIMITATION

4. The present suit is within the period of limitation prescribed under Article 113 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, which provides a period of three years from the date of accrual of the cause of action. The suit is filed well within the prescribed limitation period.

FACTS OF THE CASE

5. On 15.03.2024, the Plaintiff advanced a sum of Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- to the Defendant as a hand loan. The said amount was transferred via NEFT from the Plaintiff's bank account bearing number {{PLAINTIFF_BANK_ACCOUNT}} to the Defendant's bank account bearing number {{DEFENDANT_BANK_ACCOUNT}}, bearing UTR No. AXIB20240315123456. The proof of the said transaction is annexed herewith as **Annexure A**.

6. Following the advancement of the said loan, the Defendant acknowledged the debt liability towards the Plaintiff through digital communication on {{WHATSAPP_ACKNOWLEDGMENT_DATE}}. The copies of the said WhatsApp messages are annexed herewith as **Annexure B**.

7. There was no written loan agreement executed between the parties, and the transaction was solely based on the trust and acknowledgment evidenced by the digital communications referred to above.

PARTICULARS OF BREACH

8. The Plaintiff made repeated oral demands for the repayment of the loan amount on {{ORAL_DEMAND_DATE}} and thereafter, but the Defendant failed to comply.

9. Consequently, the Plaintiff issued a legal notice dated 01.07.2024 to the Defendant via {{LEGAL_NOTICE_DISPATCH_MODE}}, calling upon the Defendant to repay the outstanding dues. The copy of the said legal notice is annexed herewith as **Annexure C**.

10. Despite the receipt of the said legal notice and the acknowledged liability, the Defendant has failed to repay the loan amount, constituting a breach of the obligation to repay the debt. The date of default for the purpose of interest calculation is 20.06.2024.

DAMAGES

11. The Plaintiff is entitled to recover the principal sum of Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- from the Defendant, which remains due and payable.

12. The Plaintiff further claims interest at the rate of 12% per annum on the principal amount from the date of default i.e., 20.06.2024 till the date of realisation, as the Defendant has wrongfully withheld the money belonging to the Plaintiff.

LEGAL BASIS

13. The present cause is governed by Indian Contract Act, 1872, including Section 73.

14. The present cause is governed by Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, including Section 9, Section 20, Section 34.

CAUSE OF ACTION

15. The cause of action for the present suit first arose on 20.06.2024 when the Defendant committed the breach / wrongful act giving rise to the Plaintiff's right to institute the present suit.

16. The cause of action further arose when the Defendant, despite receipt of the legal notice dated {{NOTICE_DATE}}, failed and refused to comply with the Plaintiff's demand.

17. The cause of action is not a continuing cause of action. The breach consisted of a single event. However, the Plaintiff's right to damages subsists.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

18. The suit is valued at Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only) for the purposes of jurisdiction and court fee. Appropriate court fee has been paid on this plaint under the provisions of the Court Fees Act, 1870 as applicable in the State of Karnataka. The plaint is properly stamped.

INTEREST

19. The Plaintiff claims interest on the suit amount as follows:

    (a) Pendente lite interest at such rate as this Hon'ble Court deems fit and just from the date of filing of this suit till the date of judgment / decree, under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908;
    (b) Future interest at such rate as this Hon'ble Court de
